title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ulcerative colitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of the condition. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which is a hallmark of ulcerative colitis, and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and improving the balance of beneficial gut bacteria. By addressing these underlying factors, turmeric tinctures can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bleeding, offering relief from this chronic and debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Turmeric's curcuminoids, such as curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the gut, potentially reducing the severity of UC symptoms and promoting healing.

Immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric's bioactive compounds may modulate the immune system, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which can help regulate the immune response and alleviate inflammation in the gut.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC exhibits anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut, contributing to the management of ulcerative colitis.

Turmerone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, while also promoting the healing of damaged tissues and reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are also widely used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the colon.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are occasionally used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis, although their curcumin content is generally lower than that of the rhizome or roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes for tincture preparation and chop into small pieces weighing approximately 250 grams.
Combine chopped rhizomes with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar sealing it tightly for 2 weeks.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks allowing the mixture to infuse and separate.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass containers and store it in a cool dark place for 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the inflamed digestive tract. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the colon. Additionally, ginger's natural antispasmodic effects can help alleviate abdominal cramps, diarrhea, and other symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis, providing relief and improving overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the gut, which is a hallmark of ulcerative colitis.
Gut motility regulation: Ginger's carminative properties help regulate bowel movements, reducing symptoms like constipation, diarrhea, and abdominal pain common in ulcerative colitis patients.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage the gut lining and contribute to inflammation, oxidative stress, and tissue damage characteristic of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, particularly 6-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.

Shogaols: Shogaols, especially 6-shogaol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and promoting the death of damaged cells in the colon, potentially alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Zingerone: Zingerone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, potentially alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Roots: Roots are often used in tinctures as they contain a significant amount of ginger's bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ulcerative colitis.

Barks: Barks are occasionally used in tinctures for their secondary metabolites, such as gingerols and shogaols, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the gastrointestinal tract in cases of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in late autumn when the plant's foliage begins to yellow.
Clean and peel the harvested rhizomes under cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine 1 part of the prepared rhizomes with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilage extracted from the marshmallow root coats and protects the intestinal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by UC. It also has a calming effect on the gut, reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloating. By nourishing and healing the colon, marshmallow tinctures can help promote a healthy gut microbiome, leading to improved digestion and reduced inflammation in individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tincture's primary active compound, mucilage, forms a protective coating on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and irritation, and soothing symptoms of ulcerative colitis by providing a barrier against digestive acids and enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tincture help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the colon, which is characteristic of ulcerative colitis, thereby reducing damage to the gut lining and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the colon, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the gut, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
Althaea polysaccharides: These water-soluble polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the severity of inflammation in the gut and promote healing.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., althaeic acid): These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis contain mucilages that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, making them a common choice for ulcerative colitis tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.

Seeds: The seeds of Althaea officinalis are rich in mucilages and other compounds that can help to protect and heal the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:8 to 1:10 ratio of dried root material of althaea officinalis by hand or with a tool.
Chop 500g of the harvested material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root material with 1:1 ratio of 95% ethanol by volume in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional agitation.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its mucilaginous properties, which soothe and protect the intestinal lining. The herb's natural demulcent action forms a protective barrier against irritation and inflammation, reducing the severity of symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding. Additionally, slippery elm tinctures may help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing by stimulating the production of mucins, which are essential for maintaining a healthy gut lining. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for individuals suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm's mucilage property helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in slippery elm, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and pain in the colon, which can alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Demulcent properties: Slippery elm's demulcent property forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from irritation and damage caused by toxins, bacteria, or other harmful substances that can exacerbate ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which can alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Flavonoids: Exert anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the intestinal lining and reduce inflammation in the colon.
Tannins: Have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the colon, potentially providing relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: Ulmus rubra barks are used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Ulmus rubra leaves are used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Ulmus rubra roots are used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh ulmus rubra bark and 1 part of fresh ulmus rubra leaves from mature trees in late summer.
Chop the harvested ulmus rubra into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Cover the chopped ulmus rubra with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka or 35% abv in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the gut lining and reduce inflammation in the colon. The flavonoids present in licorice root also enhance the production of mucin, a protective substance that shields the gut from irritation and damage. Additionally, licorice tinctures have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of microflora and reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms associated with Ulcerative Colitis.

Soilant properties: The tannins present in licorice root have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, helping to protect and heal damaged tissues in the colon, reducing the risk of further damage and pain.

Antioxidant activity: Licorice root's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to chronic inflammation and oxidative stress in Ulcerative Colitis patients, promoting overall gut health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Liquiritin: This phenolic glycoside has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the colon from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Liquorice flavonoids (especially glabrene): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the colon, potentially alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the treatment of ulcerative colitis.
Barks: They contain a combination of saponins and flavonoids, which work synergistically to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove dirt.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar to prepare for maceration.
Fill the jar with 50% glycerin and 50% 80% ethanol mixture in a ratio of 1:2 to achieve a final tincture strength.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh or dried roots and leaves of taraxacum officinale in late spring.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale into small pieces and add 2 cups of 80 proof vodka.
Transfer the chopped taraxacum officinale mixture to a clean glass jar with a lid and seal it tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to steep.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

Seeds: Safflower seeds contain a high amount of omega-6 fatty acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help manage symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Roots: Safflower roots are a good source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the digestive tract in ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carthamus tinctorius flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried for best quality.
Chop 50 grams of fresh carthamus tinctorius flowers and leaves into small pieces for maximum extraction.
Steep the chopped carthamus tinctorius in 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol for 14 days in a dark glass container.
Strain the carthamus tinctorius tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after steeping is complete.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 30 days before using it to allow the flavors to mature.

remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain apigenin and luteolin, flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

remedy_recipe: Gather 25 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried flowers with 50 percent vodka and 50 percent distilled water in a ratio of 1:2.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks before use.

The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the colon, reducing spasms and cramping associated with IBS-C symptoms. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using a sharp blade or tool to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 50-60% vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every few days for 2-3 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 part of the chopped plant material in 2 parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality canarium album bark in late spring or early summer for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested bark thoroughly using shade drying or air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried canarium album bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle and mix with 60% vodka or everclear spirit.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Gather organic zanthoxylum americanum bark, 100-150 grams for a single batch.
Clean and dry the herb by gently brushing off any debris or dirt.
Cut the dried zanthoxylum americanum bark into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar to about 3/4 capacity.
Pour a high-proof vodka such as everclear over the herb, covering it completely, with a ratio of 1:2 (herb to liquid) by weight.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every other day.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the colon, which is a hallmark symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tincture's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage colon cells and contribute to chronic inflammation, oxidative stress, and tissue damage, which are common characteristics of ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective properties: Yarrow tincture's compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to protect the gastrointestinal lining from further damage, reducing the risk of ulcers, bleeding, and other complications associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Buds: Young buds of the yarrow plant are rich in flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that can aid in healing gut inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, ensuring 100g of plant material for the recipe.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50% ethanol or vodka in a glass jar, filling it ¾ full to allow for liquid expansion during fermentation.
Store the mixture at room temperature in a dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solvent extraction and tincture formation.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting liquid extract for long-term use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the inflamed digestive tract. The tincture's sedative effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which are common triggers for flare-ups in individuals with ulcerative colitis. Additionally, valerian's ability to regulate gut motility can help to alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal cramping. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal valerian tinctures may provide relief from the discomfort and disruption associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate symptoms of Ulcerative Colitis such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bleeding.

Gut-soothing properties: Valerian tincture can help soothe and calm the lining of the intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort, making it a natural remedy for relieving colonic spasms and cramps associated with Ulcerative Colitis.

Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) effects: Valerian root has been shown to have anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce stress levels and promote relaxation, both of which are essential for managing symptoms and preventing flare-ups in Ulcerative Colitis patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of bioactive compounds, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid and other active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from UC symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature valeriana officinalis roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for at least 30 minutes.
Chop the washed roots into small pieces and macerate them in high-proof vodka or rum at a ratio of one part root to five parts solvent for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids after four hours.
Transfer the liquid extract to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for six weeks before filtering again through a coffee filter.
Bottle the clear tincture in amber glass containers and label them with the date of preparation and recommended dosage of 20-40 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated gut linings and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The tannins present in maypop also help to heal mucous membranes, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating. Additionally, maypop's antioxidants work to protect against oxidative stress, further supporting the body's natural healing processes and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop tinctures contain compounds like mangiferin and garcinol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Antioxidant activity: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in maypop tinctures help protect the mucous membranes of the colon from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing tissue damage and promoting healing.

Gut-soothing and protective properties: Maypop's active compounds, including saponins and alkaloids, have been found to stimulate the production of mucus in the gut, which helps to soothe and protect the lining of the colon, reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing inflamed tissues and reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve gut health, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried passiflora incarnata flowers with one part vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Label and store the passiflora incarnata tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years using a 20-30 drop dosage per serving twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes inflammation in the digestive tract. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloating associated with ulcerative colitis. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to modulate the gut microbiome, promoting a balance of beneficial bacteria that can aid in the healing process. By addressing inflammation and promoting gut health, lemon balm tinctures may provide relief from the debilitating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon balm contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key factor in the development and progression of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's high antioxidant content helps protect the colon mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcers and inflammation in people with UC.

Gastroprotective compounds: Lemon balm contains gastroprotective compounds that may help reduce gastric acid secretion, decrease gut permeability, and prevent further damage to the colon mucosa, all of which are beneficial for managing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues associated with ulcerative colitis.

Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain rosmarinic acid, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain related to ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and mix with vodka at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to ensure maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles and label with date and ingredients, allowing it to mature for at least 30 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut and protect against oxidative damage. By soothing the inflamed mucous membranes and reducing inflammation, cleavers tincture may help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis, including diarrhea, abdominal pain, and rectal bleeding. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help eliminate harmful bacteria and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and cramping associated with UC.

Antioxidant activity: Cleavers contain antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which may contribute to UC development and progression, thus helping to protect the intestinal lining and reduce inflammation.

Gut-healing properties: Cleavers tincture has been traditionally used to soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the gut, which can help repair damaged tissues and improve symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and loose stools characteristic of UC.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cleavers are used to make tinctures as they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cleavers are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve gut health in cases of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems of specified dosage according to the recipe.
Cleanly wash the harvested plant material with filtered water and gently pat dry.
Combine one part plant material with three parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature, shaking daily, and filter through cheesecloth.
Store the resulting tincture in dark-tight containers at room temperature, ready for use after 6 months preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because its isoflavones, specifically formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds have been demonstrated to reduce inflammation in the colon, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools. Additionally, red clover tinctures may also promote mucosal healing and improve gut motility, leading to improved overall quality of life for individuals suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover may help modulate estrogen levels and alleviate estrogen-related symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis, such as menstrual irregularities and mood changes.

Antioxidants: Red clover's antioxidant properties can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the risk of colon inflammation and injury, which is a hallmark of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.
Flowers: The flowers contain isoflavones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality trifolium pratense dried herbs in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Clean and prepare the trifolium pratense herbs by removing stems and debris using scissors.
Combine 100 grams of trifolium pratense herbs with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ulcerative colitis are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate symptoms of the chronic inflammatory bowel disease. They help by reducing inflammation, improving gut health, and promoting digestion. The best herbal tinctures for ulcerative colitis include Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Ulmus rubra, Althaea officinalis, Terminalia chebula, Aloe barbadensis, Taraxacum officinale, Aloe vera, and Glycyrrhiza uralensis. If left untreated, ulcerative colitis can lead to painful bowel movements, weight loss, fatigue, and social isolation, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ulcerative colitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), Crohn's disease, and diverticulitis. Herbal tinctures such as peppermint, ginger, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms like diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloating. These natural remedies can be used in conjunction with conventional treatments to provide relief from chronic digestive issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with ulcerative colitis is a blend of triphala, marshmallow root, and licorice root. Triphala, a traditional Ayurvedic formula, promotes digestive health and reduces inflammation. Marshmallow root soothes and protects the intestinal lining, while licorice root helps to calm inflammation and reduce symptoms. This combination may also be enhanced with dandelion root to support liver health and reduce oxidative stress. However, consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Clinical Gastroenterology, Tormentil extracts (TE) have been evaluated for their safety, pharmacology, and clinical effects in patients with active ulcerative colitis (UC). The study involved 16 patients with active UC who received escalating doses of TE (1200, 1800, 2400, and 3000 mg/d) for 3 weeks each, followed by a 4-week washout phase. The results showed that during therapy with 2400 mg TE per day, the median Clinical Activity Index (CAI) and C-reactive protein improved significantly, indicating that herbal tinctures like TE may be beneficial for ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in these tinctures has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis. It also has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial overgrowth, a common issue in UC patients. Additionally, curcumin has been found to promote healing and reduce oxidative stress in the colon, making it a promising natural remedy for managing ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which are common symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has strong anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, which contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has shown potential in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators, which can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are commonly used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis because they contain high levels of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.

Roots: Curcuma longa roots are also used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis due to their rich content of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Barks: Curcuma longa barks are sometimes used to make tinctures for ulcerative colitis, although less commonly than roots or rhyzomes, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots in the early morning when they are at their peak potency and store them in a cool dry place.
Chop the turmeric roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio of 1 part turmeric to 2 parts vodka.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for at least six weeks to allow the mixture to macerate and infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid from the solids.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in the refrigerator to slow down the fermentation process and preserve its potency for up to two years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the breakdown of the intestinal lining. This helps to alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis, such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, by promoting healing and reducing inflammation. The tincture's anti-oxidant properties also help to protect the colon from damage caused by oxidative stress, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits in managing ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which can contribute to the development and progression of ulcerative colitis.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Terpenoids (specifically Glycyrrhetinic acid): Glycyrrhetinic acid has anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects that can help control the symptoms of ulcerative colitis and prevent tissue damage in the colon.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in treating ulcerative colitis.
Stems: They contain saponins, which may have anti-inflammatory properties that help in managing the symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of clean dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early autumn season.
Cut the dried roots into small pieces and steep them in 500 milliliters of vodka at 18% abv.
Store the steeped roots in a dark glass container with a lid for 4 to 6 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Label and store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years without refrigeration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract. The tincture's active compound, gingerol, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the gut, promoting healing and reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea. Additionally, ginger has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the colon from oxidative stress and promote overall gut health, making it a potential natural remedy for ulcerative colitis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the intestinal lining from damage and reduce inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ulcerative colitis, while also protecting the gut from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make tinctures due to their rich content of gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used in tincture production because they have been found to contain high levels of bioactive compounds that can help to regulate the gut microbiome and reduce inflammation in the colon.
remedy_recipe: Chop 250 grams of fresh root into small pieces to release its medicinal properties and prepare for extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 750 milliliters of 50% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark location for 2-3 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the solids.
Transfer the herbal tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store in a cool dark location for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. The saponins and flavonoids present in the plant have been shown to have a protective effect on the gut lining, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Additionally, Ulmus rubra has been found to have antioxidant properties, which help to combat oxidative stress and promote overall health. This makes it a promising natural remedy for managing ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Aliphatic acids: Help to reduce inflammation and soothe the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the colon, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Phenolic acids (including salicylic acid and ellagic acid): Exert anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which can help to manage symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially benefiting ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain saponins and other compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and twigs on a dry sunny day in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 1 week to prevent mold from forming.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the tincture to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the final herbal tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues in the digestive tract. The mucilages present in the tincture form a protective barrier, shielding the lining of the colon from further irritation. This reduces inflammation, alleviates symptoms of diarrhea, and promotes healing of the damaged mucosa. By promoting a healthy balance of gut flora, Althaea officinalis tinctures may also help to reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis, such as abdominal pain and rectal bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the colon, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly galactomannans, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of ulcerative colitis symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the colon, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures due to their high mucilage content, which helps in soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the colon.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in reducing inflammation in ulcerative colitis.
Barks: They are used to make tinctures due to their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of the medicinal plant in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for several weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar to prepare for infusion.
Steep the chopped roots in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts solvent for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Terminalia chebula tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compounds present in the tincture, such as chebulinic acid and chebulinic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut and promote the healing of the intestinal lining. Additionally, the tincture has been found to enhance the gut's natural barrier function, reduce oxidative stress, and modulate the gut microbiome, all of which are beneficial in managing ulcerative colitis symptoms and promoting remission.
remedy_constituents: Chebulic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, which are key factors contributing to ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Corosolic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to alleviate inflammation and modulate the immune response in ulcerative colitis.
Polysaccharides: Possess prebiotic properties, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which helps to maintain a healthy gut microbiome and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Terminalia chebula are commonly used due to their high concentration of chebulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing inflammation and healing the mucous lining in the colon.
Barks: The barks of Terminalia chebula are used for their astringent properties, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the mucous lining in the colon.
Seeds: The seeds of Terminalia chebula are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the mucous lining in the colon due to their content of various bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried terminalia chebula fruits, 750ml of 95% ethanol and a clean glass jar for storage.
Combine the dried terminalia chebula fruits with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally, to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to small glass bottles, label and store in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in the digestive tract. The tannins in aloe vera, a key component of Aloe barbadensis, have been shown to soothe irritated gut tissue and promote healing. Additionally, aloe vera's prebiotic properties support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which helps to regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. This can lead to improved symptoms and a better quality of life for individuals suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis by regulating bowel movements and promoting the healing of damaged gut tissue.
Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins help soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis by creating a barrier against irritants and toxins.

Fatty acids (specifically, linoleic acid and arachidonic acid): These fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of ulcerative colitis symptoms, such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Gel (derived from leaves): The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis contains mucilages, which can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the gut, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe barbadensis leaves with a sharp knife or pruning shears and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and weigh out a dosage of 1 part aloe barbadensis to 2 parts 80 proof vodka.
Combine the chopped aloe barbadensis with the vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because they contain dandelion root's anti-inflammatory compounds, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The prebiotic properties in Taraxacum officinale tinctures promote a healthy gut microbiome, supporting the body's natural healing processes. Additionally, the tinctures have been found to enhance the body's ability to absorb essential nutrients, contributing to the overall well-being of individuals with ulcerative colitis. This natural remedy may help alleviate symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with ulcerative colitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thus alleviating symptoms and promoting healing.
Polysaccharides: Assist in ulcerative colitis management by modulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
Saponins: Help alleviate ulcerative colitis symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving gut barrier function, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties to combat gut infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for ulcerative colitis.
Roots: The roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Flowers: The flowers have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated using clean scissors.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper bags for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discard the plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The tannins and glycoproteins in aloe vera also work to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gut, helping to alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. This natural remedy supports the body's natural healing process, promoting a healthier balance of gut bacteria and improving overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of mucosal lesions in the colon, which is beneficial for ulcerative colitis patients.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to the development and progression of ulcerative colitis.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates, such as acemannan, have been shown to stimulate the production of cytokines that help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut, which can help manage ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part to make tinctures due to their high concentration of aloin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.

Stems: Aloe vera stems are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a higher concentration of acemannan, a polysaccharide that has been shown to promote the healing of the gut lining and reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Leaves (again): Aloe vera leaves are used again due to their content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as folic acid and other B vitamins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the gut lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy skin and no signs of mold or decay.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Store the aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza uralensis tinctures helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the inflamed intestinal lining. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture reduce inflammation and promote healing, alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as abdominal pain and diarrhea. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza uralensis has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help protect the gut from oxidative stress and promote a healthy gut microbiome, further supporting the management of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the colon, thereby alleviating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Liquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing the severity of ulcerative colitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting healing of the colonic mucosa.

Isoliquiritigenin: Displays anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, thereby reducing the severity of ulcerative colitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the healing of the colonic mucosa.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and saponins that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the colon.
Stems: Rich in antioxidants that help protect the colon from oxidative stress and promote overall gut health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza uralensis roots in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 70 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days to allow for proper extraction.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the roots and achieve clarity.
